title: Satwik-Chirag Return to Spearhead India's Japan Open Campaign
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, are making their return to competitive action at the Japan Open, boosting India's medal hopes.
summary: After a brief break due to Satwik's shoulder injury, the third-seeded duo of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty are set to resume their campaign at the Japan Open, eager to pick up where they left off.

title: Haryana U-19 Tournament Postponed, Bhubaneswar Now Selection Event for World Juniors
indian_angle: This change directly impacts young Indian shuttlers aiming for selection to the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, making the Bhubaneswar event crucial.
summary: The Badminton Association of India announced the postponement of the Haryana U-19 Ranking Tournament, with the Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament now designated as the sole selection event for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026.

title: Delhi World Championships: Why Badminton Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: The article highlights the importance of engaging India's younger generations to ensure a strong future for badminton viewership and participation in the country, especially with the World Championships in Delhi.
summary: Ahead of the BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi, an analysis explores the critical need to attract Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to badminton to sustain and grow the sport's fan base in India.

title: BAI Prez Himanta Biswa Sarma: "What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game?"
indian_angle: The statement from the President of the Badminton Association of India provides a unique perspective on an unusual incident at an Indian badminton event.
summary: Himanta Biswa Sarma, President of the Badminton Association of India, commented on an incident involving monkeys at a badminton tournament, lightheartedly questioning the concern.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Adopted for Domestic Junior Tournaments
indian_angle: This significant rule change will affect the competitive landscape and player development for young Indian shuttlers in domestic ranking tournaments and national championships.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships across the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups.

title: All India Senior Ranking Tournament Set for Chennai in July
indian_angle: This upcoming tournament is a key event in the Indian domestic badminton calendar, providing a platform for senior Indian players to compete and earn ranking points.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, attracting top senior players from across the country.
